name: Ian Marrs
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary geochemistry • Mars analog processes • Habitability studies
bio: Ian Marrs is a Ph.D. student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, where he conducts research in planetary geology and geochemistry. Working under Dr. Mark Salvatore, his research focuses on understanding the chemical processes that occur in dry, rocky environments on Earth—such as deserts and volcanic regions—as analogs for similar processes on Mars. His work explores mineral formation pathways, including serpentinization, to better understand how habitable conditions and biologically relevant compounds may have developed on ancient Mars. Marrs has received competitive funding through NASA’s FINESST program and the National Science Foundation to support his research. In addition to his research, he contributes to education by teaching field-based courses that use locations like the Grand Canyon and Meteor Crater as planetary analog sites.
